subject: Social and Political Life - II
overall_learning_objectives: Understand who an MLA (Member of Legislative Assembly) is
Learn how an MLA is elected by the people
Identify the difference between the Ruling Party and the Opposition
Understand the role of the Chief Minister and other Ministers
Learn about the Legislative Assembly as a place for debate
Recognize how the government takes action through press conferences and departments
Identify the purpose of a Wallpaper Project in schools

lesson_plan.title: Who is an MLA?
topics_covered: How the state is divided into Constituencies
teaching_strategy: Storytelling based on the NCERT comic strip
Analogy: School monitor for a class vs. MLA for a constituency
Simple map showing different colored zones (constituencies)
student_activities: Find out the name of the MLA of your local area
Color a simple map showing 5 different constituencies
assessment: Oral quiz: What does MLA stand for?

lesson_plan.title: How do MLAs become Ministers?
teaching_strategy: Using a hypothetical example of 100 seats to explain 'Majority'
Explaining 'Majority' as half the number + 1
Discussion: 'What is the job of those who lose the election?'
student_activities: Solve a simple math puzzle: If a state has 60 seats, how many does a party need to win?
Identify the 'Ruling Party' in current news
assessment: Identifying the party in power from a given list of seats

lesson_plan.title: The Chief Minister and the Governor
topics_covered: Role of the Governor in appointing the CM
The Chief Minister's power to choose other ministers
teaching_strategy: Discussion: 'Who is the head of your school? Who is the head of the State?'
Explaining the symbolic role of the Governor
Mnemonic: CM = Captain of the Team
student_activities: Make a list: Name the current CM and Governor of your state
Draw a crown for the Governor and a pen for the CM to show their roles
assessment: True/False: Does the Governor run the daily government?

lesson_plan.title: A Debate in the Legislative Assembly
topics_covered: What is a Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha)?
How MLAs ask questions to the government
The process of answering and making promises
teaching_strategy: Role-play: Setting up a 'Mock Assembly' in class
Using the textbook dialogue (MLAs 1 to 6) for reading
Explaining that an MLA is responsible for their area
student_activities: Act out the NCERT debate on Patalpuram's water problem
Write one question you would ask your MLA
assessment: Oral quiz: Can only ruling party MLAs speak in the Assembly?

lesson_plan.title: Working of the Government
topics_covered: Government is not just the Assembly
Press Conferences: Explaining to the people
teaching_strategy: Newspaper clipping analysis: Finding government news
Discussion on why the CM holds a press conference
Explaining that 'Government' refers to the Ministers and Departments
student_activities: Match the Department to its job (e.g., Education - Opening Schools)
Pretend to be a reporter and write one headline for the CM's news
assessment: Short answer: What is a Press Conference?
Name two departments of the government

lesson_plan.title: Laws for the State
teaching_strategy: Simple explanation: Laws are rules for everyone
Visual aids showing a public rally/protest
Comparison: Government making laws vs. People following them
student_activities: Draw a 'Public Protest' poster for a social cause
Identify if a law (e.g., No plastic) is for the whole state
assessment: Oral quiz: Where are laws for the state made?
Exit slip: How do people show they are unhappy with a law?

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Wallpaper Project
topics_covered: Recap of MLA, CM, and Assembly
teaching_strategy: Guiding the 'Wallpaper Project' as mentioned in NCERT
Fun game: 'Who am I?' (e.g., I lead the state, I am the...)
Reviewing NCERT exercises
student_activities: Work in groups to make a 'Wallpaper' on a topic like 'Clean India'
Complete the summary chart in the notebook

teaching_aids: Social and Political Life - II Textbook
Newspaper cuttings about the CM or State Government
Political map of the state showing constituencies
Chart paper and colors for Wallpaper project
Simple flowchart showing CM $\rightarrow$ Ministers $\rightarrow$ Departments
evaluation_methods: Identifying Ruling/Opposition parties from seat counts
teacher_tip: To make 'Constituency' easy, compare the state to a giant puzzle. Each piece of the puzzle is a constituency, and each piece has its own representative (MLA). When all pieces come together, they form the government!
